title: CBOR map keys not in canonical order per RFC 8428
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c
Lines: 193-206

CATEGORY: PROTOCOL

RFC 8428 Section 6 states: 'implementations SHOULD sort keys in numeric order by the key value.'

The CBOR label values are:
  SENML_LABEL_BT = -3  (Base Time)
  SENML_LABEL_BN = -2  (Base Name)

Canonical CBOR ordering requires smaller integers first, so -3 should come before -2.

But encode_record() encodes in this order:
  Line 194: SENML_LABEL_BN (-2) first
  Line 202: SENML_LABEL_BT (-3) second

This violates the SHOULD (not MUST) for canonical CBOR ordering.

IMPACT:
- Interoperability: some strict receivers may reject or misparse
- Determinism: non-canonical encoding means identical data may produce different bytes
- Signing: if CBOR payload is signed, non-canonical form complicates verification

FIX: Reorder encoding to emit keys in numeric order:
  SENML_LABEL_BT (-3) before SENML_LABEL_BN (-2)
  Then SENML_LABEL_N (0), SENML_LABEL_U (1), SENML_LABEL_V/VB (2/4), SENML_LABEL_T (6)
notes: Fixed CBOR key ordering in encode_record() to ensure SENML_LABEL_BT before SENML_LABEL_BN per RFC 8428. Count and encoding now both follow numeric key order. Tests pass.
close_reason: CBOR map keys reordered for canonical numeric order per RFC 8428 §6. Updated count logic to match encoding order. Verified with existing test vectors.
